key_observations: Clear V-shaped recovery pattern: volume collapsed from 13.7km to 6.3km (weeks 1-4), then aggressive rebuild to 19.3km by week 6—suggests planned deload or injury/illness recovery followed by structured return-to-training

Heart rate stability improving despite increasing load: avg HR 158→160 bpm across recent weeks with pace accelerating (6:14→5:58 min/km), indicating genuine aerobic fitness gains rather than sympathetic stress
Recent sessions show elevated work capacity: last 3 runs averaging 167.8, 163.3, and 161 bpm at sub-6:00 pace suggests lactate threshold improvements and readiness for intensity work
warnings: HR data missing for first 4 weeks limits early fatigue assessment; cannot confirm whether initial volume drop was planned or reactive
Only 10 tracked activities over 8 weeks (1.25 runs/week average)—suggests cross-training or non-Strava activity; training structure unclear without complete picture
Recent acceleration (15.7→19.3km + 3 runs/week) over just 1 week is aggressive; monitor for overtraining signals in week 7
recommendations: Maintain current 3 runs/week frequency but cap weekly volume at 20-22km for next 2 weeks to consolidate fitness gains before next progression; risk of injury is elevated with rapid rebuilding
Incorporate one dedicated threshold/tempo session weekly (currently missing from data); your recent easy-run paces and stable HR suggest readiness for structured intensity
Reintroduce HR monitoring on all runs to build aerobic baseline data; current 3-week sample is too small to establish personal zones and progression metrics

weeks_analysis.assessment: Single 13.7km run at 6:14 pace; solid aerobic effort but incomplete weekly structure. No HR data limits assessment.
29% volume drop suggests deload or recovery week; consistent 6:11 pace maintained despite lower volume. Data gap of 2 weeks unexplained.
Continued descent to lowest point (47% below week 1); single easy 7.2km at 6:12 pace. Strong signal of planned taper or return-from-injury protocol.
Nadir reached at 6.3km; pace improved to 5:47 despite minimal volume—suggests readiness for progression phase. Still single run, incomplete structure.
Key inflection point: 3 runs, volume +150%, avg HR 158 bpm. Include football activity (158 avg HR). Rebuild phase initiated with controlled intensity.
Strong progression: +23% volume, 3 dedicated runs at avg 160 bpm, paces 5:58-5:59 min/km. Elevated HR trend (+2 bpm) is appropriate for workload increase. Fitness rising but fatigue accumulating.
